channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Turkish minister says an attack on aerospace company left a number of people dead
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey's interior minister said an attack on the premises of the Turkish state-run aerospace and defense company TUSAS on Wednesday left a number of people dead or injured. Ali Yerlikaya did not provide further details on the attack on Turkish Aerospace Industries Inc.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US defense chief says there’s evidence North Korea has sent troops to Russia
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The U.S. defense secretary said Wednesday there is evidence that North Korea has sent troops to Russia, calling it a “very, very serious issue” if they join the war in Ukraine on Moscow's side and warning of possible consequences.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G7 allies are moving ahead with a $50 billion loan for Ukraine backed by frozen Russian funds
item.description: The White House says Group of Seven allies are moving forward with providing Ukraine with $50 billion in loans for Ukraine backed by frozen Russian assets.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: As Romania prepares to phase out coal by 2032, some miners are retraining for jobs in renewable energies.
item.dc:creator: Anca Gurzu, Cipher News, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Charles gets a warm welcome on his first visit as king to Samoa. But uneasy questions await
item.description: SIUMU, Samoa (AP) — In the dark, dozens of people lined a narrow road near the Samoan fishing village of Siumu, gathering near a sign bearing a portrait of King Charles III and waiting excitedly for a glimpse of the real monarch.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY and AYAKA McGILL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Turkish minister says an attack on aerospace company left a number of people dead
item.description: Turkey’s interior minister says an attack on the premises of the Turkish aerospace and defense company TUSAS has left a number of people dead or injured.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Group of metal detectorists uncover hoard of coins in England
item.description: A group of metal detectorists discovered a hoard of 21 coins near Okehampton Castle nearly a year ago, but the news of their finding was just recently unveiled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon chief: North Korean troops are in Russia
item.description: Secretary of Defense Lloyd Austin said North Korean troops appear to be in Russia. “We are seeing evidence that there are North Korean troops that have, that have gone to … Russia,” Austin told reporters in Rome on Wednesday. “What exactly they’re doing, left to be seen.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Belarus sets a Jan. 26 election that's almost certain to extend its authoritarian leader's rule
item.description: Lawmakers in Belarus set the next presidential election for Jan. 26, a vote almost certain to extend the three-decade rule of authoritarian leader Alexander Lukashenko.
item.dc:creator: Yuras Karmanau, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump campaign files FEC complaint over Labour staff campaigning for Harris
item.description: Former President Trump’s campaign team filed a complaint with the Federal Election Commission on Monday, labeling the U.K. Labour Party’s efforts to support Vice President Harris as “election interference.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes pound Lebanese coastal city after residents evacuate
item.description: Israeli jets have struck multiple buildings in Lebanon’s southern coastal city of Tyre, sending large clouds of black smoke into the air.
item.dc:creator: Mohammed Zaatari And Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Britain and Germany sign defense pact to counter Russia's growing threat
item.description: LONDON (AP) — German submarine-hunting planes will patrol the North Atlantic from a base in Scotland under a new Britain-Germany defense pact sealed in response to the growing threat from Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: US scrambles after Israel war plan leak: What to know
item.description: An apparent U.S. intelligence breach related to Israel's plans to strike Iran has unsettled the intelligence community and sent the Biden administration scrambling to determine if it came from a hack or an internal leak.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Indigenous Australian Senator Lidia Thorpe intensifies criticism of King Charles III
item.description: CANBERRA, Australia (AP) — An Indigenous senator has intensified her criticism of King Charles III, again accusing the British monarch of complicity in the “genocide” against Australia’s First Nations peoples and declaring on Wednesday she will not be “shut down.” Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sensational mass trial shines a dark light on rape culture in France
item.description: The sensational trial of 50 men accused of raping an unwitting and unconscious French woman who was repeatedly drugged by her husband has exposed what critics say is a dark rape culture in the country.
item.dc:creator: Diane Jeantet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Villagers are wary of plans to dam a river to ensure Panama Canal's water supply
item.description: EL JOBO, Panama (AP) — A long, wooden boat puttered down the Indio River’s chocolate waters carrying Ana María Antonio and a colleague from the Panama Canal Authority on a mission to hear directly from villagers who could be affected by plans to dam the river.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: It could take 350 years for Gaza to rebuild if it remains under a blockade, UN report says
item.description: United Nations agencies have long warned that it could take decades to rebuild Gaza after Israel's offensive against Hamas, one of the deadliest and most destructive military campaigns since World War II. Now, more than a year into the war, a new report speaks in terms of centuries. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: About 140 Rohingya Muslims on wooden boat off Indonesia's coast as residents refuse to let them land
item.description: LABUHAN HAJI, Indonesia (AP) — About 140 weak and hungry Rohingya Muslims, mostly women and children, were on a wooden boat anchored about 1 mile (0.60 kilometers) off the coast of Indonesia’s northernmost province of Aceh on Tuesday, officials said, and local residents refused to allow them onto la
item.dc:creator: BINSAR BAKKARA and EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police bust restaurant serving ‘best-selling’ pizza with side of cocaine
item.description: German police have raided a Düsseldorf pizzeria for allegedly serving pies with a side of cocaine. The restaurant's 36-year-old manager was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea warns it can send arms to Ukraine after reports of North's troops in Russia
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea warned Tuesday it could consider supplying weapons to Ukraine in response to North Korea allegedly dispatching troops to Russia, as both North Korea and Russia denied the movements. NATO's secretary general said that would mark a “significant escalation.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blinken urges Netanyahu to seek truces in Lebanon, Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony J. Blinken met with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday to discuss ending the conflict in Lebanon and Gaza in addition to securing pathways for humanitarian aid, according to a readout from the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Woman wedged between boulders in Australia for hours
item.description: The woman had apparently dropped her phone and was trying to retrieve it while hiking in Australia, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, NewsNation

item.title: Ancient Roman statue discovered by construction workers in Bulgaria
item.description: During construction work in the city of Varna, workers found a well-preserved marble statue dating back to the Roman era outside the fortified walls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken arrives in Israel as Biden-Harris admin faces scrutiny over compromising Jerusalem’s security
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken arrived for talks with Israel on his 11th trip there since October 2023. The Jewish state is fighting a seven-front war against Iran-backed proxies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah naval base in Beirut as Iran rallies Gulf Arab nations
item.description: Hezbollah launched missiles on central Israel Tuesday. Meanwhile, Israel Defense Forces said it struck a Hezbollah naval base in Beirut, along with other terrorist targets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Peru’s ex-president gets 20+ years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea demands withdrawal of North Korean troops allegedly helping Russia fight Ukraine
item.description: South Korea issued a demand to Russia to condemn the cooperation between Russia and North Korea, calling for the withdrawal of North Korean troops from Russia's war with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Austin warns 'no silver bullet' to defeat Putin as US aid hangs in balance ahead of elections
item.description: Secretary of Defense Austin said there is "no silver bullet" to defeat Russian President Putin and urged the global community to bolster aid to Ukraine as concern mounts over continued U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF releases video showing Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar fleeing into tunnel with family before Oct. 7 attack
item.description: The IDF released footage showing Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar fleeing underground into a tunnel in Gaza in the hours leading up to the Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sanders: Trump will be 'closer to Netanyahu' on Gaza if reelected
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-Vt.) said Sunday he believes former President Trump will have similar views of the Israel-Hamas war to those of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he is reelected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Reddit user discovers mysterious 'doorway' in Antarctica
item.description: "Monsters Inc." bedroom doors, and an underground bunker were more suggested explanations, some in jest others not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba gets some electricity back after major power outage left millions in the dark
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Some electricity was restored in Cuba, the government said Saturday, after the island nation's worst blackout in at least two years left millions without electricity for two days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba's power grid fails, plunging country into darkness
item.description: Cuba's government closed schools and non-essential industries and sent most state workers home in a last-ditch effort to keep the lights on for residents.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: No slowing of US government contracts for Raytheon amid Qatar bribery charges
item.description: Under the agreement, the company will have to demonstrate good conduct for the next 3½ years and foster a culture of ethics and compliance with anti-corruption laws.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation staff, NewsNation
